steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Both terms have the same variable part, $x^3$, so subtract only the coefficients: $$14x^3-6x^3=(14-6)x^3$$

steps.title: Keep the exponent
steps.content: The exponent stays $3$ because like terms are being combined, not multiplied: $$(14-6)x^3=8x^3$$ Choice B adds the coefficients, and choices A and D incorrectly change the exponent. The answer is C.
